zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Masters I於1395年出生在英國英格蘭肯特維也斯波洛時，他的父親John Masters21歲，母親Mrs. John Masters18歲。他於1416年與Joanne Webb在英國英格蘭肯特福利頓登聖 馬也斯 邱奇結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1455年5月22日在英國賀福爾享年60歲。

nameMeanings.name: Masters
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational and occupational name from Middle English (atte) maisteres ‘(at the) master's (house)’, a name for someone who lived or worked there, normally a servant. Compare Master and Masterman , and for the type compare Maidens , Monks , Parsons .
variant of Master , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Master
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Amar, Dhimant, Pravin, Amit, Aruna, Bhanu, Gaurang, Gopal, Haren, Jagu, Jayesh, Kalpesh.

English: occupational name from Middle English maister ‘master’ (Old French maistre). The word had a wide range of applications, and the reference in surnames may be to a schoolmaster, to the master of a farm or house, to the master of an apprentice, or to a ship's captain. In Scotland the eldest sons of barons and the uncles of lords were called Masters, but this is not primarily a Scottish surname.
German: dialect form of   Meister   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Maidens
meanings: occupational or habitational name from Middle English (atte) maydenes ‘(at the) maiden's or maidens' (house)’, a name for someone who lived or worked at such a house. Compare Masters and Parsons .
post-medieval variant of Maiden , with excrescent -s.

variants.name: Marsters
meanings: English: variant of Masters . Marsters is the predominant surname on Palmerston Island, Cook Islands, South Pacific, deriving from a settler and owner reputedly born in Leicestershire.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Masters I was born in 1395, in Willesborough, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Masters, was 21 and his mother, Mrs. John Masters, was 18. He married Joanne Webb in 1416, in St Mary's Church, Frittenden, Kent, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 22 May 1455, in Herefordshire, England, at the age of 60.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Masters I(이)가 1395년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 윌레스버루그h에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Masters(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. John Masters(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1416년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 프리텐든 스트 마이'스 c후르치에서 Joanne Webb(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1455년 5월 22일 영국 허퍼드실르에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Joanne Webb в 1416 г. в местности ст мэри'с чурч, фриттенден, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 22 мая 1455 г. в местности хирефордшир, Англия в возрасте 60 лет.
